Motion For Order Re Identification Of Defendant Shila Acharya; Memorandum Of Points And Authoritites; Affidavit (Declaration) Of Identity In Support; Exhibits Thereto; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar May-04-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Labor Commissioner's Motion for Order Re Identification of Defendant Shila Acharya is GRANTED.

Plaintiff presents an affidavit of identity in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 680.135 which shows Defendant Shila Acharya also uses the names Shila Thapa Acharya and Bhagabati T Acharya. (See Sagar Affidavit, pars. 1-40; Exs. A-J.) That is the only reasonable inference from the adduced real property and banking documents. The court notes that if no such relationship existed, defendant could simply prepare a declaration to that effect. Defendant did not do so, nor does she submit any other opposing evidence.

Defendant's other arguments lack merit. Labor Code section 98(h) is inapposite because Plaintiff is not seeking to amend the judgment to "conform" to defendant's legal name. Rather, Petitioner is adding additional names. Code of Civil Procedure section 674 does not apply because plaintiff is not seeking an abstract of judgment. Defendant references her appeal of this court's order denying her motion to vacate judgment for lack of proper service. But the appeal does not divest this court of jurisdiction and an undertaking must be provided to stay enforcement of a money judgment. (Code of Civil Procedure section 917.1.) Lastly, defendant cites no authority holding that the other names needed to be used in connection with her employment dealings or that the addition of the names is necessary to enforce the judgment.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-25-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Palyn Hung, Esq. 415-265-8716 Labor Commisioner's Office for Plaintiff.
Jon Webster, Esq. 925-686-8790 for Defendant.
Court reporter: Victoria Thomas, CSR #12927, 559-623-3367, vlthomascsr@gmail, reported

ARGUED. Mr. Webster sent a timely notice to contest and presents arguments. Mr. Webster raise issues on: client is a natural person, reasonable diligence to effect service. Ms Hung responds; adverse witness, substitute service is complete, there is a judicial comment. Mr. Webster presents last words. The Court poses questions and shares its views. After full argument, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT SHILA ACHARYA's Motion To Set Aside Judgment.

Taken Under Submission.***

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Reported. (302/JMQ)

After the hearing, an order on defendant shila acharya's motion to vacate is signed. (see scanned order for details)

MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LABOR COMMISSIONER, STATE OF CALIFORNIA HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

Off calendar at parties' request.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Jul-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - PLAINTIFF LABOR COMMISSIONER, STATE OF CALIFORNIA HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. (Marta Gebreslasie)

Petitioner Labor Commissioner's Motion For Order Determining Marta Gebreslasie's Claim Of Exemption is GRANTED and Gebreslasie's Claim of Exemption is DENIED.

Good cause appearing, the court grants Petitioner Labor Commissioner's Motion For Order Determining Marta Gebreslasie's Claim Of Exemption. And Gebreslasie failing to have sustained his burden to establish a relevant exemption and the record otherwise not supporting an exemption as to the levied bank account funds, Gebreslasie's claim of exemption is denied.

2 orders are signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
